title: AUC-Safe Multi-Expert Blend With Purged Group-Time CV
summary: The strongest public guidance here is that calibration can change ROC AUC if isotonic introduces ties, while grouped and temporal splits are needed to avoid optimistic leakage.
rationale: The strongest public guidance here is that calibration can change ROC AUC if isotonic introduces ties, while grouped and temporal splits are needed to avoid optimistic leakage. A blend of orthogonal experts is more promising than adding yet more correlated handcrafted features to one model.
implementation_hint: Validate with race-level GroupKFold plus a forward holdout or TimeSeriesSplit-style year ordering and a small gap/embargo around held-out races if constructing lagged encodings. Blend binary, ranker, and hazard models using OOF rank-average or sigmoid/temperature scaling only; avoid isotonic unless AUC is checked explicitly.
expected_effect: Should improve leaderboard transfer by selecting only stable experts and preserving ranking quality during calibration.
risk: Stricter CV may lower local CV immediately, making good ideas look worse unless model selection is stability-weighted instead of single-fold-score driven.
